Issues: Metadata

Metadata differences:

FieldCH02CFrank OemigUTG
DescriptionHL7-defined table of codes used to specify how important the most important waiting mesasge is. For example, if there are 3 low priority messages, 1 medium priority message and 1 high priority message, the message waiting priority would be "high", because that is the highest priority of any new message waiting.HL7-defined code system of concepts which specify the disposition of the patient at time of discharge (i.e., discharged to home, expired, etc.). Used in HL7 Version 2.x messaging in the MSA segment.HL7-defined code system of concepts which specify the disposition of the patient at time of discharge (i.e., discharged to home, expired, etc.). Used in HL7 Version 2.x messaging in the MSA segment.

FieldCH02CFrank OemigUTG
DescriptionHL7-defined table of codes used to specify at each receipt the status of the container in which the specimen is shipped in chain of custody cases where specimens are moved from lab to lab. If the container is compromised in any way (seal broken, container cracked or leaking, etc.), then this status needs to be recorded for legal reasons.HL7-defined code system of concepts used to specify at each receipt the status of the container in which the specimen is shipped in chain of custody cases where specimens are moved from lab to lab. If the container is compromised in any way (seal broken, container cracked or leaking, etc.), then this status needs to be recorded for legal reasons. Used in the Specimen (SPM), Shipment (SHP) and Shipment Package (PAC) segments in HL7 Version 2.x messaging.HL7-defined code system of concepts used to specify at each receipt the status of the container in which the specimen is shipped in chain of custody cases where specimens are moved from lab to lab. If the container is compromised in any way (seal broken, container cracked or leaking, etc.), then this status needs to be recorded for legal reasons. Concept Domain Analysis

In HL7 V2, a concept domain is an abstract classification that defines the semantic space for a set of coded values, without itself containing a concrete list of codes. Some CH02C table entries are pure concept domains — they reference an external or user-defined coding scheme rather than enumerating codes directly.

356 tables are identified as concept-domain-only entries in CH02C.

Concept Domains with CodeSystems in Other Sources (1)

These tables are marked as concept-domain-only in CH02C, yet have concrete CodeSystem resources in UTG and/or Frank Oemig’s IG. This may indicate a mismatch in how the table is classified versus how it is implemented.

TableNameFrankUTG
0133 [THO]Procedure practitioner identifier code type✗✓
Observation: When a concept domain has concrete CodeSystem resources in UTG or Frank Oemig’s IG, it suggests either that the CH02C classification as concept-domain-only may be outdated, or that the CodeSystem resources represent a binding to a specific value set rather than a full enumeration of the domain. These cases merit review to ensure consistent treatment across all three sources.
↑ Back to top

Annex: Methodology

This annex describes the methods used for each type of analysis in this report.

Table Identification and Matching

  • CH02C tables are identified by their 4-digit table number from the V2.9.1 Word document (Chapter 2C).
  • Frank Oemig’s FHIR IG CodeSystems are matched via the codesystem-tableNo extension, which contains the 4-digit table number.
  • UTG CodeSystems are matched via the v2-XXXX pattern in their FHIR resource ID and canonical URL (e.g., http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v2-0001).
  • The v2-tables.xml catalog (628 entries) was also loaded as a reference for the complete set of V2 tables UTG recognizes.

Code-Level Comparison

  • Codes are matched by exact, case-sensitive string comparison of their code values.
  • CH02C codes are extracted from the codedContent arrays in the Word document tables.
  • Frank’s codes come from FHIR concept[].code elements in his JSON CodeSystem resources.
  • UTG codes come from FHIR concept[].code elements in XML CodeSystem resources.
  • A code is reported as “missing” from a source if no exact string match is found.

Status Value Normalization

  • CH02C uses single-letter status codes: D (Deprecated), B (Backward-compatible), N (New), R (Retired), A (Active).
  • Frank’s IG and UTG use full-word FHIR-style values: inactive, backward, new, retired, active.
  • Before comparison, statuses are normalized: D→deprecated, B→backward, N→new, R→retired, A→active, inactive→deprecated.
  • A mismatch is reported when the normalized values differ between any two sources.

Display Name Comparison

  • Display names are compared case-insensitively after trimming whitespace.
  • If one display is a case-insensitive prefix of another (e.g., “Admit/Visit” vs “Admit/Visit Notification”), it is classified as a “truncation” rather than a substantive difference.
  • This distinction helps separate intentional abbreviations from genuine naming disagreements.

Metadata Comparison

  • Code System OIDs are compared by exact string match.
  • Symbolic names are compared case-insensitively.
  • Descriptions are compared case-insensitively after trimming, excluding values of “TBD”.
  • Table type (HL7, User, External, etc.) is recorded from CH02C for reference but not compared across sources.
↑ Back to top
